question_text: What is a moment in physics?

options.label: A
options.text: The turning effect of a force around a pivot

options.label: B
options.text: The speed at which an object moves

options.label: C
options.text: The amount of force needed to lift an object

options.label: D
options.text: The distance an object travels in one second

correct_answer: A) The turning effect of a force around a pivot
explanation: A moment is the turning effect produced when a force acts on an object at a distance from a pivot point. It makes things rotate or turn around a fixed point, like a seesaw turning around its centre.
